title: What did my grandmother believe about money?
subtitle: A journalling set for mapping the oldest layer of the money story you inherited.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for tracing the money beliefs of the women furthest back in your line: what they believed, what drove those beliefs, what they passed on, and what you are choosing to take forward and what to leave behind. Five prompts, twenty to thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2 prompts on breaking generational money patterns.

practice.whenToUse: Use when doing deeper work on the ancestral layer of the money pattern, or after a ritual or tapping session on forgiveness.

content.text: A journal set for reaching back to the oldest layer of the inherited money story. Use "grandmother" loosely: the specific woman, or whoever in your line held the beliefs that have carried farthest forward. Five prompts. Allow twenty to thirty minutes.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Who is the woman furthest back in your line you know anything about?
Name her if you can, or describe her. What era did she live in? What were her circumstances?
What do you know about her relationship with money?
What you were told, what you saw, what you infer from the stories.
What did she believe about money? Write the belief as a statement she might have said.
"Money doesn't grow on trees." "Women don't need to know about money." "We don't talk about money." Write her version.
What would you want her to know, looking back from where you stand?
Write it as a letter sentence. One to three lines. What you've learned that she didn't have access to.
What from her do you take forward? What do you leave behind?
Her resilience, her frugality, her care for the household — whatever is worth keeping. Name it alongside what you are choosing to leave.
Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2, which addresses the ancestral layer of inherited money patterns.
